name: Set Workflow
description: Automated workflow: Set Workflow. This workflow integrates 13 different services: webhook, stickyNote, wait, scheduleTrigger, merge. It contains 29 nodes and follows best practices for error handling and security.

nodes.name: OpenAI

values.content: You are a gentle and professional Chinese medicine practitioner who provides health advice in a friendly, encouraging tone. Please generate a response in JSON format with the structure {"message": "..."}, keeping the message brief (<100-200 words), persuasive, reminding me to drink water, clearly specifying intervals (such as 2 hours), and mentioning at least one benefit of drinking water (such as replenishing qi) and one negative effect of dehydration (such as blood stasis), encouraging me to take action to drink water, ending with an action prompt. Start directly without using any form of address.

nodes.name: Sticky Note2

nodes.notes: This stickyNote node performs automated tasks as part of the workflow.

nodes.name: Sticky Note3

parameters.content:
## When User interact the drink button. Record the drink value to sheet and send back the iOS health log water url to start the shortcut

**Note for Shortcut:**

The shortcul url will be like `shortcuts://run-shortcut?name=darrell_water&input=%7B%22value%22%3A100%2C%22time%22%3A%222025-03-04T16%3A10%3A15%22%7D`

It's url encoded. The decoded version will be:
`shortcuts://run-shortcut?name=darrell_water&input={"value":100,"time":"2025-03-04T16:10:15"}`

We can see it pass the shortcut name and input with json string value. This will be used in iOS shortcut

nodes.name: Edit Fields-Set progress

assignments.name: progress_image
assignments.value: ={{ (function() {    let p = $json.sum_value / $json.target;    let n = Math.round(p * 10);    n = Math.max(0, Math.min(10, n));    return '💧'.repeat(n) + '⬜'.repeat(10 - n);  })() }}

values.content: You are a gentle and professional Chinese medicine practitioner who provides health advice in a friendly, encouraging tone. Please generate a response in JSON format with the structure {"message": "..."}, keeping the message brief (<100-200 words), persuasive, reminding me to drink water, clearly specifying intervals (such as 2 hours), and mentioning at least one benefit of drinking water (such as replenishing qi) and one negative effect of dehydration (such as blood stasis), encouraging me to take action to drink water, ending with an action prompt. parameters.content: If already drink recently. Delay the notification in 3x minutes randomly
## Send the slack notification with AI wording. Also have the drink water action buttons

## When User interact the drink button. Record the drink value to sheet and send back the iOS health log water url to start the shortcut

**Note for Shortcut:**

The shortcul url will be like `shortcuts://run-shortcut?name=darrell_water&input=%7B%22value%22%3A100%2C%22time%22%3A%222025-03-04T16%3A10%3A15%22%7D`

It's url encoded. The decoded version will be:
`shortcuts://run-shortcut?name=darrell_water&input={"value":100,"time":"2025-03-04T16:10:15"}`

We can see it pass the shortcut name and input with json string value. This will be used in iOS shortcut

prerequisites: An active n8n instance installed locally, in a Docker container, or hosted on n8n Cloud.
Basic familiarity with importing JSON templates in n8n.
setupInstructions: Download the JSON configuration for this workflow.
Ensure you set up credentials for all connected integrations.
Test your trigger and run execution.
requiredCredentials: open Ai  A P I details
google Sheets  O Auth2  A P I details
slack  O Auth2  A P I details
configurationSteps: Open the scheduler node "Schedule Trigger".
Adjust the execution intervals (e.g., Every hour, Daily, or Custom CRON expression) according to your preferences.
Locate the webhook node named "slack drink webhook".
Copy the Test Webhook URL provided by n8n.
Configure your source application (e.g. Stripe, Typeform) to send event payloads to this URL.
Open the "OpenAI" node (n8n-nodes-base.noOp) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Limit" node (n8n-nodes-base.limit) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Google Sheets - Get Target" Google Sheets node.
Ensure you select the spreadsheet file and specific tab/sheet from the dropdown menus.
Map the input JSON keys to your target sheet columns.
Open the "Summarize" node (n8n-nodes-base.summarize) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "combine data" node (n8n-nodes-base.merge) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "If Node" node (n8n-nodes-base.if) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Wait" node (n8n-nodes-base.wait) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note1" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note2" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note3" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note4" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Slack send drink notification" Slack node.
Select the Channel or User ID where message notifications should be delivered.
Open the "Send to Slack with confirm" Slack node.
Select the Channel or User ID where message notifications should be delivered.
Open the "Google Sheets - log water value to sheet" Google Sheets node.
Ensure you select the spreadsheet file and specific tab/sheet from the dropdown menus.
Map the input JSON keys to your target sheet columns.
Open the "Google Sheets - Get Today Water Log" Google Sheets node.
Ensure you select the spreadsheet file and specific tab/sheet from the dropdown menus.
Map the input JSON keys to your target sheet columns.
Locate the Set/Edit fields node "slack_action_payload".
Define or edit values for variables that are referenced in downstream nodes.
Locate the Set/Edit fields node "slack_action_drink_data".
Define or edit values for variables that are referenced in downstream nodes.
Locate the Set/Edit fields node "Edit Fields-Set progress".
Define or edit values for variables that are referenced in downstream nodes.
testingSteps: Click the "Test step" or "Listen for test event" button on your Trigger node.
Send a test trigger from your source service (e.g. fill a test form or trigger a webhook trigger).
Verify that the node canvas lights up green, indicating successful data flow.
Open the final destination node to confirm that output records were saved correctly.
instructions.expectedResult: Your Slack channel will receive customized real-time notifications when the automation executes.
troubleshooting: Check credential warning symbols. Most workflow issues stem from expired OAuth connections or missing API permissions.
Verify that the data keys coming from your Trigger node match the exact names mapped in subsequent nodes (e.g., {{ $json.body.email }}).
Open the Execution history tab in n8n to view logs and determine which node failed.
securityNotes: Never hardcode API keys, passwords, or authentication bearer tokens in the plain text fields of node parameters.
If you download or share this workflow JSON, sanitize any sensitive parameters or configuration nodes.
customizationIdeas: Add an email node at the end to notify you of successful executions.
Incorporate an AI LLM node to summarize trigger payloads before writing them to integrations.
